My 2023 workflow for making a website in under 5m
- Create a new Nuxt app with `npx nuxi init`
- Publish the repo to GitHub with vscode
- Connect the GitHub repo to @Netlify for auto-deploys

As AI continues to expand, at what point does tricking it into revealing information differ from a skilled interviewer & careless subject?

I think of myself as a hacker-journalist, but that means a lot of different things

A coder who does research
A dev who knows storytelling

is hacking is just exposing information people don't want to be public?

does security need to be breached for it to be a "hack" anymore?
"hacking" is such a loaded & ambiguous word that you don't even really have to do the work that it implies to make your target look weak
I like this new trend of "hacking" as just collecting a bunch of publicly-available documents, grouping them just so, and writing a pastebin

Many people just wanted an anti-establishment candidate: between Bernie and Hillary, it was clear who represented the establishment. I wonder what those people think now that Trump has been in power 4 years.

As anecdotal as the rest of them, but I drove back and forth across the country from Jan 20, 2016 to late March and I talked to a bunch of Trump supporters, and a surprising number of them said variations of "It's a shame what happened to Bernie, I would have voted for him"
I think that Bernie Sanders is the candidate most likely to get votes from people who voted for Trump in 2016.
